codeSystems.name: USCoreConditionCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Condition Codes
codeSystems.description: This describes the problem. Diagnosis/Problem List is broadly defined as a series of brief statements that catalog a patient's medical, nursing, dental, social, preventative and psychiatric events and issues that are relevant to that patient's healthcare (e.g., signs, symptoms, and defined conditions). ICD-10 is appropriate for Diagnosis information, and ICD-9 for historical information.

codeSystems.name: USCoreProcedureCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Procedure Codes
codeSystems.description: Concepts from CPT, SNOMED CT, HCPCS Level II Alphanumeric Codes, ICD-10-PCS,CDT and LOINC code systems that can be used to indicate the type of procedure performed.

NEMSIS ePatient.25 - The patient's sex as recorded by EMS.
Used for documentation and mapping to Patient.gender and the us-core-birthsex extension.
Note: ePatient.13 (Gender) is DEPRECATED in NEMSIS 3.5.1.251001CP2 — use ePatient.25 (Sex) instead.

codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eSituation.20 - The clinical reason for an interfacility transfer or transport.

codeSystems.name: EMSTransportDelayVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Type of Transport Delay
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eResponse.11 - Types of delay during transport.

codeSystems.name: EMSTransportDispositionVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Transport Disposition
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.30 - Whether/how the patient was transported.

codeSystems.name: EMSTransportMethodVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Transport Method
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.16 - The vehicle/mode used to transport the patient.

codeSystems.name: EMSTransportModeVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Transport Mode from Scene
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.17 - Emergent vs non-emergent transport mode from scene.

codeSystems.name: EMSTraumaTriageHighRiskVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Trauma Triage High Risk VS
codeSystems.description: Trauma triage criteria indicating high risk for serious injury — CDC Field Triage Guideline red criteria (injury patterns and physiologic instability) (eInjury.03).

codeSystems.name: EMSTraumaTriageModerateRiskVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Trauma Triage Moderate Risk VS
codeSystems.description: Trauma triage criteria indicating moderate risk for serious injury — CDC Field Triage Guideline yellow criteria (mechanism of injury and EMS judgment) (eInjury.04).

codeSystems.name: EMSTurnAroundDelayVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Type of Turn-Around Delay
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eResponse.12 - Types of delay returning to service after transport.

codeSystems.name: EMSUnitDispositionVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Unit Disposition
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eDisposition.27 - Whether/how the unit made patient contact.

codeSystems.name: EMSUnitTypeVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Unit Type (ValueSet)
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eResponse.07 - The type of EMS vehicle/unit.

codeSystems.name: EMSVascularAccessLocationVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Vascular Access Location
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS eProcedures.13 - Anatomical site of vascular access (IV/IO line).

codeSystems.name: EMSVehicleTypeVS
codeSystems.title: EMS Vehicle Type
codeSystems.description: NEMSIS dVehicle.04 - The type of EMS vehicle.

codeSystems.name: EMSWorkInjuryExposureVS
codeSystems.title: EMS WorkInjuryExposure VS
codeSystems.description: Type of crew work-related injury, death, or suspected exposure (eOther.06).
